content.plain_english: This week your child is counting all the way to 100 and writing two-digit numbers. They are getting more confident saying which is bigger and which is smaller.
content.in_school: Children build numbers with bundles of ten and single ones. They count in 1s, 2s, 5s and 10s. They place numbers on a long number line from 0 to 100 and find one more, one less, ten more and ten less.
content.home_activity: On the walk to school, take turns calling out two-digit numbers you see — door numbers, bus numbers, prices in shop windows. Ask your child to say the number of tens and ones. Then pick two numbers and ask which is bigger.
dinner_table_questions: Can you count from 50 to 100 with me?
What number is ten more than 67?

key_vocabulary.definition: A number written with two digits — like 47.
Look at two numbers to see which is bigger or smaller.
A line with numbers in order, used for counting and comparing.

example_questions: Order these from smallest to largest: 35, 53, 13, 31.
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Counting in 10s from 0 to 100 takes 10 steps — count them on your fingers.
